seo.title: PII Filtering for AI Agents in Instructor Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix pii filtering for ai agents in Instructor agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Instructor, PII Filtering for AI Agents manifests as structured extraction failures where sensitive data like emails, phone numbers, or SSNs leak into validated Pydantic models despite filtering attempts. The framework's client patching and validation retries often conflict with PII redaction logic, causing 'Structured output validation retries exhausted' when models generate PII-contaminated responses. This is exacerbated by Instructor's partial extraction mode, where required fields may contain masked PII fragments that fail schema validation.

howItManifests.scenario: Email extraction in user profile parsing
howItManifests.symptom: Model returns 'john.doe@example.com' in validated EmailStr field despite PII filter
howItManifests.impact: PII leak through structured output; validation passes unexpectedly

howItManifests.scenario: Phone number parsing from customer support logs
howItManifests.symptom: Structured output validation retries exhausted after 3 attempts with redacted numbers
howItManifests.impact: Agent crashes; conversation halts waiting for valid phone field

howItManifests.symptom: Partial extraction missing required fields due to over-aggressive PII masking
howItManifests.impact: Incomplete customer data; downstream systems receive null addresses

howItManifests.scenario: SSN field in financial document processing
howItManifests.symptom: Model hallucinates enum values like 'XXX-XX-1234' outside PII-safe schema
howItManifests.impact: Data compliance violation; audit failures and regulatory fines

fixes.name: Pre-Filter Prompt Injection
fixes.description: Inject PII filtering instructions directly into Instructor's system prompt before structured extraction
fixes.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from openai import OpenAI
import pydantic

client = from_openai(OpenAI())

class SafeUserProfile(pydantic.BaseModel):
    name: str
    email: str = pydantic.Field(..., description="NEVER include real emails. Use 'REDACTED@example.com'")
    phone: str = pydantic.Field(..., description="ALWAYS mask phone numbers as 'XXX-XXX-XXXX'")

SYSTEM_PII_FILTER = """
CRITICAL: Before extracting structured data:
1. Replace ALL real emails with 'REDACTED@example.com'
2. Replace ALL phone numbers with 'XXX-XXX-XXXX'
3. Replace ALL SSNs with 'XXX-XX-XXXX'
4. Never invent real-looking PII
"""

def safe_extract_user_data(message: str):
    return client.messages.create(
        model="gpt-4o-mini",
        messages=[
            {"role": "system", "content": SYSTEM_PII_FILTER},
            {"role": "user", "content": message}
        ],
        response_model=SafeUserProfile,
        max_retries=1  # Reduce retries since we're filtering upfront
    )

fixes.name: Custom Pydantic PII Validator
fixes.description: Add PII-safe validators to Instructor's Pydantic models to reject contaminated extractions
fixes.code:
import pydantic
import re
from instructor import from_openai
from openai import OpenAI

client = from_openai(OpenAI())

def no_pii_validator(v: str) -> str:
    pii_patterns = [
        r'\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b',
        r'\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b',
    ]
    for pattern in pii_patterns:
        if re.search(pattern, v):
            raise ValueError(f"PII detected: {v[:20]}... Use REDACTED format")
    return v

class SafeContact(pydantic.BaseModel):
    name: str
    email: str = pydantic.Field(..., validator=no_pii_validator)
    phone: str = pydantic.Field(default="XXX-XXX-XXXX", validator=no_pii_validator)

# Now Instructor will reject PII-contaminated responses
response = client.messages.create(
    model="gpt-4o-mini",
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Extract: john.doe@gmail.com (555)123-4567"}],
    response_model=SafeContact
)

fixes.name: Instructor Response Interceptor (TS)
fixes.description: TypeScript middleware that scrubs PII before Instructor validation completes

vexAutomation.heading: Automating PII Filtering for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automates PII detection in Instructor agents by wrapping `client.chat.completions.create()` with `vex.verify()`, continuously monitoring structured outputs for PII leakage patterns across all validation retries. It detects the four common failure modes—exhausted retries, partial extraction gaps, hallucinated enum values, and nested inconsistencies—using Instructor-specific signal extraction from Pydantic validation errors and response objects.

Vex's remediation engine automatically applies context-aware fixes: injecting PII-safe system prompts for your specific schema, adding custom Pydantic validators, or implementing response interceptors without code changes. It learns from your Instructor usage patterns, adapting to new PII types and extraction schemas while maintaining <1% latency overhead.

Integration takes 3 lines of code and catches 98% of PII leaks missed by manual regex scanning, with automatic rollback to safe fallbacks when validation fails.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex import verify
from instructor import from_openai
from openai import OpenAI
import pydantic

client = from_openai(OpenAI())

class UserProfile(pydantic.BaseModel):
    name: str
    email: pydantic.EmailStr
    phone: str

@verify(problem="pii-filtering-instructor")  # Vex auto-detects Instructor + PII issues
async def extract_profile(message: str) -> UserProfile:
    return client.messages.create(
        model="gpt-4o-mini",
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": message}],
        response_model=UserProfile,
        max_retries=3
    )

# Vex automatically:
# ✅ Injects PII filtering prompts
# ✅ Adds safe validators to your models
# ✅ Monitors all 4 failure modes
# ✅ Applies fixes without code changes

result = extract_profile("Extract profile: john.doe@gmail.com (555)123-4567")

# Vex dashboard shows: PII detected → Auto-fixed → 100% safe extraction
